page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-88-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-heavy server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8481C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 88 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 44 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 704.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The processor runs at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz and includes a 105 MB L3 cache. For local storage, the instance bundles 12 NVMe SSDs totaling 38,652 GB of local capacity, while containing zero GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 72,975.63 and a Passmark CPU score of 71,866.87. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5,960.52 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 99.47 GB/sec. In database and web serving workloads, the server demonstrates high throughput, registering 80.68 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 74.72 GB/sec in static web serving. Additionally, it shows top-tier performance in Geekbench image processing and strong capabilities in multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the z3-highmem-88-highlssd offers a dense consolidation of memory and high-capacity local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external storage attachments for data-intensive tasks. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions enhances its efficiency for CPU-based deep learning and cryptographic operations without requiring dedicated GPU hardware. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data analytics pipelines, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-88-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-intensive server instance designed for demanding enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 88 dedicated vCPUs and 44 physical cores, it features 704.0 GB of RAM and 38,652 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 12 drives. The instance lacks dedicated GPUs but leverages advanced CPU instructions, including Intel AMX and AVX-512, to accelerate specialized compute tasks. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database throughput, with Redis SET operations reaching 80.68 million operations per second. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency by combining massive local storage with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-88-highlssd is an x86_64 server with 88 vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and 38,652 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for large-scale databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-88-highlssd features 88 vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and 38,652 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and local NVMe storage instance designed for massive database workloads and high-throughput data processing.
bullet_points: 88 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz.
704.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.
38,652 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 12 bundled drives.
Strong multi-core performance with 80.68 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix math.
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and heavy ETL pipelines.
